source: Verified specifications from Telecaster '72 Custom Deluxe. Scale 647.7mm, radius 241.3mm, neck 20mm/22mm, bolt-on joint confirmed from spec sheet 2026-03-29. First Fender instrument in spec library.
notes: The Telecaster (1950) is the oldest mass-produced solid body electric guitar still in production. Bolt-on maple neck, ash or alder body, single-coil pickups. The longer 647.7mm scale and rounder 241.3mm fretboard radius are defining Fender characteristics distinguishing it from all Gibson instruments in this library.

72_custom_deluxe.description: Fender Telecaster '72 Custom Deluxe — reissue of 1972 variant with wide-range humbuckers. Double-bound body, maple neck. Representative of the modern Telecaster standard dimensions.

neck.depth_note: Neck-only depth. Add fretboard thickness (~6mm for Fender maple board) for total stack.
neck.fretboard_radius_note: 9.5 inch radius is rounder (more curved) than Gibson 12 inch. Vintage Fenders used 7.25 inch. Modern players often prefer 9.5-12 inch.
neck.neck_joint_note: 4-bolt neck plate. Bolt-on allows neck removal for adjustment or replacement — key serviceability advantage over set neck.
neck.neck_angle_note: Telecaster uses a flat (0 deg) neck angle. String height achieved via bridge saddle adjustment and neck shimming if required.

headstock.angle_note: Fender headstock is flat (0 deg) — no headstock angle. String trees used on B and high-E strings to create break angle over nut.
headstock.string_tree_note: Required on flat headstocks to maintain break angle on treble strings.

hardware.bridge_note: Original Tele uses 3 brass barrel saddles (pairs of strings per saddle). Modern variant uses 6 individual saddles for precise intonation.
hardware.pickup_note: 72 Deluxe uses Seth Lover wide-range humbuckers — physically sized like single coils but wired as humbuckers.

standard.description: Fender Telecaster Standard — classic single-coil configuration. Neck and body dimensions identical to 72 Deluxe. Different pickup and bridge hardware.

thinline.description: Fender Telecaster Thinline — semi-hollow Telecaster body with f-hole. Same neck as Standard. Lighter weight.

comparison_to_gibson_explorer.scale_length_note: Telecaster is 19mm longer scale — noticeable tension difference, brighter tone
comparison_to_gibson_explorer.fretboard_radius_comparison: Tele 241.3mm (9.5in) is more curved than Explorer 304.8mm (12in) — Tele feels rounder under fingers
comparison_to_gibson_explorer.neck_joint: Tele bolt-on vs Explorer set neck — different sustain and serviceability
comparison_to_gibson_explorer.headstock_angle: Tele 0 deg vs Explorer 14 deg — requires string trees on Tele
comparison_to_gibson_explorer.fret_count: Tele 21 vs Explorer 22 — one less fret
